# Test & benchmark fixtures

Shared image fixtures, deliberately spanning the input types codecs behave
differently on. Used by the e2e suite (`tests/e2e/`) and the benchmark
(`benchmarks/`).

| File | What | Source |
|------|------|--------|
| `photo.jpg` | Real photograph, 1024Ă—683 (lots of colour / high entropy) | downloaded from picsum.photos (seed `photo`) |
| `photo-large.jpg` | Big photograph, 4000Ă—3000 (12 MP) â€” stress / "do big images differ?" | downloaded from picsum.photos (seed `large`) |
| `illustration.png` | Flat shapes, ~6 solid colours, sharp edges (lossless-friendly; exposes JPEG ringing) | generated by `generate.mjs` |
| `transparent.png` | RGBA with a full 0â†’255 alpha range (opaque disc + alpha-gradient band) â€” alpha tests | generated by `generate.mjs` |
| `gradient.png` | 512Ă—512 smooth diagonal+radial gradient, **no dither** | generated by `generate.mjs` |
| `gradient-dithered.png` | 512Ă—512 â€” the *same* gradient with 4Ă—4 ordered (Bayer) dithering | generated by `generate.mjs` |
| `hard-edges.png` | 512Ă—512 high-contrast B/W concentric rings + checker quadrant (zone-plate-like) | generated by `generate.mjs` |
| `noise-synthetic.png` | 512Ă—512 full-frame seeded pseudo-random RGB noise (incompressible) | generated by `generate.mjs` |
| `screenshot.png` | 1280Ă—800 synthetic UI: title bar, panels, bitmap-font text rows, 1px rules | generated by `generate.mjs` |

### What each new fixture isolates

| File | Axis / characteristic | Differentiates |
|------|-----------------------|----------------|
| `gradient.png` | Smooth tones, **banding** under 8-bit quantisation | Separates 8-bit lossy (WebP-lossy, MozJPEG) which band, from AVIF / JPEG XL which hold gradients cleanly. |
| `gradient-dithered.png` | Same gradient + dither **noise** | Paired with `gradient.png` to isolate *banding vs. noise*: the only difference is the dither, so size/quality deltas attributable to the gradient vs. the added high-frequency noise can be read directly. Dither defeats palette/lossless wins and adds entropy. |
| `hard-edges.png` | **Ringing** on sharp high-contrast edges | Lossy DCT codecs (MozJPEG, WebP-lossy) ring/mosquito around the rings; lossless and JPEG XL stay clean. The zone-plate pattern also probes high-frequency handling. |
| `noise-synthetic.png` | **Incompressible** worst case | QOI catastrophe; exposes each lossless codec's overhead floor (output â‰ˆ raw). Output size is near-constant, so it doubles as a **regression canary** â€” a sudden size change flags a codec/encoder behaviour change. |
| `screenshot.png` | **Text + flat fills + sharp 1px edges** | JPEG XL (modular) and WebP-lossless win on crisp UI content; AVIF-lossless is comparatively weak here. Stresses what real screenshots look like without a photo. |

## Regenerating the synthetic fixtures

```sh
node tests/fixtures/generate.mjs
```

Deterministic (pure math, no deps) â€” regenerates every synthetic fixture
(`illustration`, `transparent`, `gradient`, `gradient-dithered`, `hard-edges`,
`noise-synthetic`, `screenshot`) byte-for-byte identically. Anything that needs
"randomness" (the noise field, dither) uses a hardcoded-seed `mulberry32` PRNG â€”
never `Math.random()`/`Date.now()`, which aren't available in all contexts and
would break determinism. The screenshot's text is drawn with a hardcoded 5Ă—7
bitmap font (no font dependency). The photographic fixtures are real downloaded
photos (synthetic noise isn't representative of real photo entropy); the picsum
seeds are fixed so they're reproducible too.

## Notes

- Why multiple types: a codec change can improve photos but hurt flat
  illustrations, break alpha, or behave differently on huge inputs. The fixtures
  cover all four of those scenarios so a regression in any of them is caught.
- Swap `photo.jpg` / `photo-large.jpg` for your own images for article-quality
  numbers; if you do, re-capture the benchmark baseline.

## Recommended future additions (deferred)

Two real photos from [imagecompression.info](https://imagecompression.info)
("New Test Images") would round out the corpus with real-sensor characteristics
that synthetic fixtures can't fully mimic:

- **`nightshot_iso_1600`** â€” high-ISO sensor grain (real luminance/chroma noise,
  unlike the uniform synthetic noise; stresses how codecs handle film-like grain).
- **`spider_web`** (or `leaves_iso_200`) â€” extreme high-frequency detail
  (separates codecs on fine-texture retention).

Each would be downscaled to ~1500px wide. **Deferred for now**: the site only
distributes these inside a single large `rgb8bit.zip` archive (the per-image
PPMs aren't individually fetchable â€” the tried direct paths 404), and decoding
PPM + downscaling cleanly would need an image dependency this generator
deliberately avoids. If added later, they must be downscaled/altered and carry
the licence notice: *"derived from imagecompression.info New Test Images,
downscaled/altered, redistributed under their grant."*
